Items.Key: format string vulnerability in format_string template function|format string vulnerability|cwe-134|medium|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|218|223|string_utils::format_string|template<typename... args> inline std::string format_string(const char* fmt, args... args) { char buffer[buf_size]; snprintf(buffer, buf_size, fmt, args...); return buffer; }|the fmt parameter is passed directly to snprintf as the format string. if caller passes user-controlled data as fmt, format specifiers can leak stack data or cause crashes.|any call where fmt argument contains attacker-controlled format specifiers like %s, %x, %n|use snprintf(buffer, buf_size, "%s", fmt) or validate fmt contains no format specifiers before use->format string vulnerability in format_string template function|format string vulnerability|cwe-134|medium|0,75|enhanced_calc.cpp|218|223|string_utils::format_string|template<typename... args> inline std::string format_string(const char* fmt, args... args) { char buffer[buf_size]; snprintf(buffer, buf_size, fmt, args...); return buffer; }|the fmt parameter is passed directly to snprintf as the format string. if caller passes user-controlled data as fmt, format specifiers can leak stack data or cause crashes. note: function is defined but not invoked in current source.|any call where fmt argument contains attacker-controlled format specifiers like %s, %x, %n|use snprintf(buffer, buf_size, "%s", fmt) or validate fmt contains no format specifiers before use

buffer overflow in evaluate_expression with unchecked strcpy|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|835|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); }|when expression length >= 1024 bytes (stack_buffer_size), strcpy is used without bounds checking, overflowing the 1024-byte stack buffer input_buffer_ and enabling stack corruption.|input expressions of 1024 characters or longer|use safe_string_copy for all cases or check bounds before strcpy; reject expressions exceeding buffer size->buffer overflow in evaluate_expression with unchecked strcpy|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|835|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); }|when expression length >= 1024 bytes (stack_buffer_size), strcpy is used without bounds checking, overflowing the 1024-byte stack buffer input_buffer_ and enabling stack corruption.|input expressions of 1024 characters or longer|use safe_string_copy for all cases or check bounds before strcpy; reject expressions exceeding buffer size

command injection in execute_admin_command via system()|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|user-controlled command string is passed directly to system(). attacker can execute arbitrary os commands by injecting shell metacharacters (e.g., 'exec rm -rf /' or 'exec; cat /etc/passwd').|authenticated admin user sending 'exec <malicious_command>' via admin interface|remove system() call entirely, or use a whitelist of allowed commands with strict input validation and avoid shell interpretation->command injection in execute_admin_command via system()|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|user-controlled command string is passed directly to system(). attacker can execute arbitrary os commands by injecting shell metacharacters (e.g., 'exec rm -rf /' or 'exec; cat /etc/passwd').|authenticated admin user sending 'exec <malicious_command>' via admin interface|remove system() call entirely, or use a whitelist of allowed commands with strict input validation and avoid shell interpretation

command injection in append_override_audit_event via system()|command injection|cwe-78|medium|0,85|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; system(command.c_str());|environment variable temp/tmpdir is concatenated into a system() command. if environment is attacker-controllable, command injection is possible via path traversal or shell metacharacters.|emergency_override authentication path execution with attacker-controlled environment|use c++ file i/o (std::ofstream) to write audit logs instead of shell commands->command injection in append_override_audit_event via system()|command injection|cwe-78|medium|0,85|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; system(command.c_str());|environment variable temp/tmpdir is concatenated into a system() command. if environment is attacker-controllable, command injection is possible via path traversal or shell metacharacters.|emergency_override authentication path execution with attacker-controlled environment|use c++ file i/o (std::ofstream) to write audit logs instead of shell commands

truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 identified 15 vulnerabilities but missed 8 true vulnerabilities (SC-V3-012, SC-V3-014, SC-V3-015, SC-V3-016, SC-V3-017, SC-V3-018, SC-V3-019, SC-V3-020) and misclassified severity/CWE on several findings. All 12 found vulnerabilities had partial or full coverage. Two findings (format_string template function not invoked, command injection in append_override_audit_event focused on wrong CWE) are partially overclaimed.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Format string vulnerability in log_debug_message" with evidence "std::string active_format = runtime_log_format(); printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the format string vulnerability in log_debug_message where runtime_log_format() result is passed as format string to printf. The vulnerability exists and was found.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Integer overflow in factorial computation" with evidence "long long result = 1; for (long long i = 2; i <= static_cast<long long>(x); i++) { result *= i; }"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified integer overflow in factorial computation. The vulnerability exists at lines 315-318.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Use-after-free in stage_deferred_history_flush with pool cleanup" with evidence "char* scratch = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(copy_len + 1)); ... memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup()"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the use-after-free where memory is allocated from pool, used in async lambda, then cleanup() frees all pool memory.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Command injection in validate_config via system()" with evidence "std::string validator_cmd = get_setting("validator_path", "/usr/bin/validator"); std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file; int result = system(command.c_str());"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified command injection in validate_config where user-controlled settings are concatenated into system() call.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Buffer overflow in evaluate_expression with unchecked strcpy" with evidence "if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified stack buffer overflow in evaluate_expression. Also found related issue in safe_string_copy (dest[src_len] when src_len == dest_size).

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Race condition in ThreadSafeCounter due to read outside lock" with evidence "long long old_value = value_; std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); value_ = old_value + 1;"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the race condition where value_ is read outside the mutex lock in ThreadSafeCounter::increment.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Authentication backdoor via EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE" with evidence "if (login_attempts_ > 5) { if (input.find("EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE") != std::string::npos) { authenticated_ = true;"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the logic bomb/backdoor in authentication. The vulnerability exists at lines 592-598.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Path traversal vulnerability in ConfigLoader::load_config" with evidence "std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename; if (!std::filesystem::exists(full_path)) return false;"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified path traversal in ConfigLoader::load_config where filename is concatenated without validation.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Buffer overflow in ExpressionParser::parse via unchecked strcpy" with evidence "buffer_size_ = 256; expression_buffer_ = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(buffer_size_)); ... strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified heap buffer overflow in ExpressionParser::parse where 256-byte heap buffer is used with unchecked strcpy.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Hardcoded administrative secret in source code" with evidence "constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH";"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified hardcoded credentials at line 73.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Weak session token generation easily brute-forced" with evidence "std::mt19937 gen(rd()); std::uniform_int_distribution<> dis(1000, 9999);"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified weak PRNG for session tokens using mt19937 with only 4-digit output (1000-9999).

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A - not found in Run 2
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify the thread-unsafe static localtime cache. Source lines 230-231: 'static std::tm cached_time; if (auto* local_time = localtime(&time_t)) cached_time = *local_time;' - localtime is not thread-safe and the static cached_time is shared across threads.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Command injection in append_override_audit_event via system()" with evidence "const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("TEMP"); std::string audit_path = ... + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; system(command.c_str());"
Items.Notes: Run 2 identified the append_override_audit_event function but focused on command injection (CWE-78) rather than insecure temporary file handling (CWE-377). The vulnerability involves using environment-controlled TEMP/TMPDIR paths in system() calls, which is primarily a CWE-377 issue with secondary CWE-78.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A - not found in Run 2
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify persistent authentication state (CWE-613). The class maintains authenticated_, login_attempts_, and session_token_ as instance variables that persist across operations without proper lifecycle management.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A - not found in Run 2
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify unhandled exception in handle_variable_command. Line 729: 'double value = std::stod(tokens[3]);' can throw std::invalid_argument or std::out_of_range if tokens[3] is not a valid number, and this is not caught.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A - not found in Run 2
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify integer underflow in MemoryPool::allocate. Lines 132-133: 'constexpr size_t SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES = 64; size_t remaining = previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES;' - if previous_size < size + SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES, this underflows (though guarded by 'should_split = previous_size > size', the subtraction can still underflow if size + 64 > previous_size).

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A - not found in Run 2
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify unbounded recursion in ExpressionParser. parse_expression calls parse_term which calls parse_factor which on PAREN_OPEN calls parse_expression recursively (lines 381-431). No depth limit is enforced despite MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH being defined in config.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A - not found in Run 2
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify data race in result_cache_. ExpressionEvaluator::evaluate_cached accesses result_cache_ without holding cache_mutex_ (lines 445-451), and schedule_expression_analysis enqueues tasks via thread_pool_->enqueue that can run concurrently.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A - not found in Run 2
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify TOCTOU in ConfigLoader::load_config. Lines 543-546: 'std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename; if (!std::filesystem::exists(full_path)) return false; std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(25)); std::ifstream file(full_path);' - the exists check and file open are separated by a sleep, creating a TOCTOU window.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A - not found in Run 2
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify integer division by zero in calculate_route_score. Line 752: 'return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length);' - if token_length is 0 (empty input), this causes division by zero.
